strategic_objective: Clarify whether the strong class separation in Destination Port is a stable, genuine attack signal or a dataset artifact and assess its risk as a shortcut feature.
key_checks: Verify class-conditioned distribution stability of Destination Port across temporal and data partitions
Measure cardinality and entropy of Destination Port within each class to detect near-constant or low-variation patterns
Assess shortcut-like predictive leverage of Destination Port alone for DDoS class
Check for abrupt distributional shifts or near-deterministic mappings in Destination Port values
success_criteria: Evidence that Destination Port distributional shift is consistent and not an artifact
Identification of potential brittleness or overfitting risk due to reliance on Destination Port
Characterization of Destination Port as a localized representation-sensitive feature or shortcut
router_constraints: preserve evidence refs related to e33
